Going in Style (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $45,018,541Details
International Box Office $40,200,000
Worldwide Box Office $85,218,541
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,953,050
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,420,791
Total $8,373,841
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Lifelong buddies Willie, Joe and Al, decide to buck retirement and step off the straight-and-narrow for the first time in their lives when their pension fund becomes a corporate casualty Desperate to pay the bills and come through for their loved ones, the three risk it all by embarking on a daring bid to knock off the very bank that absconded with their money.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$11,932,330 (26.5% of total gross)
Legs:3.77 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:52.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$24,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,061 opening theaters/3,076 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $60,024,716

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 7th, 2017 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: April 5th, 2017 (Wide) (Netherlands)
April 6th, 2017 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
April 7th, 2017 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
April 7th, 2017 (Wide) (Argentina)
April 7th, 2017 (Wide), released as Seni Lapinai (Lithuania)
Video Release: July 11th, 2017 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for drug content, language and some suggestive material.
(Rating bulletin 2423 (Cert #50396), 5/4/2016)
Running Time: 96 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Remake
Plot point: Autumn Years, Heist, Money Troubles, Revenge, White Collar Crime
Social setting: Crime Caper, Retirement
Subgenre: Crime Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: New Line Cinema, Village Roadshow Productions, De Line Pictures, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Lifelong buddies Willie, Joe and Al, decide to buck retirement and step off the straight-and-narrow for the first time in their lives when their pension fund becomes a corporate casualty Desperate to pay the bills and come through for their loved ones, the three risk it all by embarking on a daring bid to knock off the very bank that absconded with their money.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$11,932,330 (26.5% of total gross)
Legs:3.77 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:52.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$24,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,061 opening theaters/3,076 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $60,024,716
